Japanese: ６月２１日　午前　９時４８分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 21, 9:48 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ええええっ！
English: Whaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: かるまけんじ‥‥って、 あの‥‥？
English: Prosecutor von Karma!? You mean...?

Japanese: いや、それが‥‥ どうも、その２代目らしいよ。
English: No, I heard it's his successor this time...

Japanese: にだいめ‥‥。
English: Successor...?

Japanese: ヒドいヤツだったよね。 狩魔検事。
English: Manfred von Karma. He was a really sinister man.

Japanese: 自分が勝つためなら、 どんなヒキョウな手だって‥‥
English: He pulled all sorts of nasty tricks; all so he could win.

Japanese: （“カンペキ”という コトバに異常にこだわる男‥‥）
English: (He was a man obsessed with the word "perfection"...)

Japanese: （記録上は、４０年間 ‥‥完全に無敗）
English: (He had a perfect record... for forty long years.)

Japanese: （有罪判決のウラで、いったい どんな汚い手が使われたことか）
English: (Who knows what sorts of dirty tricks he used to get each of those guilty verdicts?)

Japanese: （‥‥２代目、か。いったい、 どんなヤツなんだろう）
English: (And now, his successor... I wonder what kind of person they will turn out to be...)

Japanese: もうダメだあ‥‥。
English: It's no good...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまっ！
English: Mystic Maya!!

Japanese: はみちゃん！
English: Pearly!

Japanese: よく来てくれたねー。 ありがとう。
English: You showed up! Thanks for coming all this way!

Japanese: だってわたくし、 もうシンパイで‥‥。
English: I was really worried about you...

Japanese: あれ？　おばさまは‥‥ いっしょじゃないの？
English: Hey, where's your mother? Didn't you two come together?

Japanese: おかあさまは、 修験者たちを見ています。
English: Mother is watching over the trainees.

Japanese: ２日つづけて修行を お休みすることはできない、って。
English: She said they have training for two days straight with no breaks.

Japanese: え！　じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ ひとりで？
English: Huh!? Then... Then... You came all by yourself?

Japanese: はい！　お屋敷を抜け出して、 地図を見ながら。
English: Yup! I snuck out of the manor and followed a map.

Japanese: もしかして、歩いてきたの？
English: Don't tell me you walked all the way here...

Japanese: まさか。 ‥‥もちろん、走ってきました！
English: Of course not! ...I ran!

Japanese: そ、それはそれは‥‥。 （電車で２時間かかるんだぞ‥‥）
English: That's... I can't... Oh my... (If it takes two hours by train... Oh man...)

Japanese: はみちゃん。 あの‥‥電車は？
English: Pearly... What about the train?

Japanese: はい？ “でんしゃ”‥‥ですか？
English: Huh? What's a... "tray-in"?

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだこりゃ）
English: (I give up...)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、お時間ですね。
English: ...It's time, isn't it?

Japanese: うう‥‥コワいなぁ、カルマ検事。 何するかわかんないもん。
English: Um... I'm really scared... What if Von Karma tries to do something to me?

Japanese: あたしやっぱり、 御剣検事がよかったよ。
English: At least I know Mr. Edgeworth would be nicer to me than Von Karma!

Japanese: みつるぎけんじさん‥‥って、 どなたですか？
English: Mr. Eh-ji-werth? Who is that?

Japanese: んーとね。なるほどくんの ライバル。‥‥でも、親友なの。
English: Um, he's Nick's rival... Well, he's also a friend.

Japanese: いやー、今でもハッキリ おぼえてるよ！
English: I still remember him as if I had just seen him only yesterday.

Japanese: 最後のサイゴまで、それは それはタイヘンな裁判ばっかり。
English: Every trial was a scorching fierce battle until the very end.

Japanese: ‥‥とまあ、こんなカンジでさ。 宿命のライバルだよねー‥‥
English: It was always back and forth with them. But when you're rivals for life...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！ ‥‥その名前は口にしないで。
English: Maya! Please don't mention that name ever again...

Japanese: ‥‥ど‥‥ どうしたの？　なるほどくん‥‥
English: Huh...? But why, Nick?

Japanese: ご‥‥ごめん。‥‥真宵ちゃんは 知らなかったかもしれないね。
English: I'm... I'm sorry, Maya. I forgot you don't know...

Japanese: アイツは‥‥もう、 いないんだよ。どこにも。
English: He... He's... He's gone... And he's not coming back...

Japanese: えええええッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ちょちょ、ちょっと待ってよ！ そ、それってどういう‥‥
English: W-W-Wait... Wait a second!! What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: 開廷時間です！ 法廷へ入ってください！
English: Court will commence shortly. Please proceed into the courtroom.

Japanese: 行こう。今は そんな話をしてるときじゃない。
English: Let's go. Now's not the time to talk about that anyway.

Japanese: な‥‥、なるほどくん‥‥？
English: N...Nick...?

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: June 21, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: これより、綾里　真宵の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Maya Fey.

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側とも、 準備のほうはよろしいですかな？
English: Are the prosecution and the defense prepared?

Japanese: （なんだ、この女の子は‥‥）
English: (What is with this girl...?)

Japanese: コラッ！　準備は できてるのですか！　成歩堂くん！
English: *Ahem!* Mr. Wright!! Are you finally prepared!?

Japanese: え！
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、す、スミマセン‥‥。 （なんでぼくばっかり怒るんだよ）
English: A-Ah, yes, Your Honor. (Why does he always seem mad at me!?)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 私が女なので、おどろいている。 ‥‥そんなところね。
English: You must be a little shocked because I am a woman, correct?

Japanese: （‥‥ってことは、やっぱり この子が、狩魔検事の‥‥）
English: (...Hold on. So this kid is the "famed" Prosecutor von Karma...?)

Japanese: 私の名は狩魔　冥（かるまめい）。 ‥‥天才検事よ。
English: I am Franziska von Karma, the Prodigy.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: I... see...

Japanese: 私がアメリカの検事局を捨てて この国へ来た理由は、ただ１つ。
English: I gave up a promising career in Germany and came to this country for one sole reason.

Japanese: ‥‥復讐よ。
English: Revenge.

Japanese: ‥‥復讐‥‥？ （父親、狩魔　豪のことか‥‥）
English: Revenge...? (Is this about her father, Manfred von Karma...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥個人的なことならば、 のちほどゆっくりと‥‥
English: Um... If it's something of a personal nature, I'm sure you can...

Japanese: あうッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥発言中よ。
English: I'm talking.

Japanese: ジャマすると、 このムチがモノを言うわ。
English: If you interrupt again, my whip will do the speaking for me.

Japanese: （ムチじゃなくて クチでモノを言ってくれぇ‥‥）
English: (Please speak with your mouth like a normal person... I beg of you...)

Japanese: いてッ！
English: Yeow!!

Japanese: ‥‥私はかならず、あなたを倒す！
English: Make no mistake; I will defeat you!

Japanese: カクゴすることね‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Prepare to go down, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: では、狩魔検事。 ‥‥冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Prosecutor von Karma, your opening statement, please...

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔の血を引く者は、 １つの宿命を負っている。
English: Those of Von Karma blood have only one fate.

Japanese: それはすなわち、 “カンペキな立証”‥‥
English: And that is "perfection".

Japanese: 被告人・綾里　真宵に 罪を逃れる道はないと宣言するわ！
English: The defendant, Maya Fey, will find no escape from her guilt on my watch.

Japanese: わわ、わかりました。 ‥‥ところで、弁護人。
English: V-Very well... What is the defense's position?

Japanese: はい。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側の主張ですが、《完全無罪》 ということでいいのですか？
English: Does the defense wish to enter a plea of "not guilty"?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥バカほどバカゆえに バカなユメを見るもの‥‥。
English: Foolish fool who foolishly dreams of foolish dreams...

Japanese: 今から１０分以内。弁護人は、 意見を変えざるをえなくなる。
English: Ten minutes. I give the defense ten minutes before it changes its plea.

Japanese: そう。《正当防衛》という 逃げ道にね。
English: That's right. I'll have you running for the "justified self-defense" plea in no time.

Japanese: （《正当防衛》‥‥）
English: ("Justified self-defense"...)

Japanese: （命が危険にさらされたとき、 自衛のため相手を死なせること）
English: (A plea usually reserved for when a person unintentionally kills in defense of himself.)

Japanese: （たしかに、そのほうが 証明しやすいけど‥‥）
English: (We could very easily make a solid case that it was self-defense, but...)

Japanese: 弁護側は、あくまでも 《完全無罪》を主張します！
English: The defense stands by the plea of "not guilty", Your Honor!

Japanese: （《正当防衛》は、“殺した”と 認めてしまうわけだからな）
English: (Because to plead "justified self-defense" is to say you did kill someone.)

Japanese: ‥‥おろかな。
English: How foolish.

Japanese: じゃあ、最初の証人を呼ぶわ。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: If that's how you want to play it, Mr. Phoenix Wright,
then I shall now call the first witness.

Japanese: （‥‥オヤジとちがって イキがいいなあ‥‥）
English: (She's just as scary as her father. Like father, like daughter I suppose.)

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業は？
English: Witness, your name and occupation?

Japanese: はッ！　名前は糸鋸　圭介。 所轄署の刑事ッス。
English: Yes, sir! My name is Dick Gumshoe. I'm a detective at the local precinct.

Japanese: うはっ！
English: Aah!!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: Get to the point already.

Japanese: さっさと事件の説明をなさい。
English: Explain to the court the details of this murder.

Japanese: りょ、リョーカイッス！　では、 現場の上面図を見てもらうッス。
English: Y-Yes, sir! Um, if everyone would please look at this map.

Japanese: 《対面の間》には窓がなく、 扉にはカギがかかっていたッス。
English: The Channeling Chamber has no windows and the door was locked shut.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、部屋の中には 被害者と被告しかいなかったッス。
English: At the time of the murder, only the victim and the defendant were in the room.

Japanese: ２人はそこで、 何をしていたんですか？
English: What were they doing in there?

Japanese: はッ。‥‥その。 ちょっと、霊媒をッスね‥‥。
English: Um... They... Well, they were channeling... a spirit... Sir.

Japanese: れれ‥‥れいばい‥‥？
English: Ch-Channeling a spirit??

Japanese: （裁判長、ウサンくさそうな カオしてるな‥‥）
English: (The look of disbelief on the judge's face is...)

Japanese: ‥‥えへん。 とにかく、霊媒が始まって数分後、
English: *ahem* Anyway, a few minutes after the channeling started,

Japanese: 部屋の中から銃声がしたッス。
English: gunshots were heard coming from inside the room, sir.

Japanese: ドアをぶっコワして、 数名の証人が部屋に踏みこむと‥‥
English: A few of the witnesses broke the door down, and rushed into the room.

Japanese: 被害者はすでに 亡くなっていた、と。
English: Ah, and that's when they found that the victim was already dead, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上ないぐらい 明白な事件ですな。
English: Hmm, I believe this is one of the most open and shut cases I have ever presided over.

Japanese: 証拠品《上面図》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Floor Plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それで‥‥被害者は どうやって殺害されたのですか？
English: So, how was the victim killed?

Japanese: それが‥‥ 少々こみいってるッス。
English: I was about to get to that...

Japanese: さっさとそれを証言なさい。
English: Stop wasting my time, then.

Japanese: ～被害者の死因について～
English: -- Cause of Death --

Japanese: 直接の死因は、コメカミに 撃ちこまれたピストルの弾丸ッス。
English: The direct cause of death was a pistol shot to the forehead, sir.

Japanese: 弾丸は、かなり至近距離から 撃たれているッス。
English: The shot was fired from point-blank range.

Japanese: ただ、被害者は撃たれる前に、 ムネを刺されているッス。
English: But before the victim was shot, sir, he was stabbed in the chest.

Japanese: 傷は、かなりヒドかったッスが、 即死にはいたらなかったッス。
English: The wound was very severe, but not enough to cause instantaneous death.

Japanese: 犯人は、トドメを刺すために ピストルを使ったッス。
English: The murderer used the pistol to finish the victim off after the stabbing.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ピストルで撃たれる前に、 刺されているんですか‥‥。
English: So the victim was stabbed before being shot...

Japanese: これが被害者の解剖記録ッス。
English: This is the victim's autopsy report, sir.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 受理します。 ‥‥では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: The court accepts it into evidence. Mr. Wright, you may question the witness.

Japanese: ～被害者の死因について～
English: -- Cause of Death --

Japanese: 直接の死因は、コメカミに 撃ちこまれたピストルの弾丸ッス。
English: The direct cause of death was a pistol shot to the forehead, sir.

Japanese: 弾丸は、かなり至近距離から 撃たれているッス。
English: The shot was fired from point-blank range.

Japanese: ただ、被害者は撃たれる前に、 ムネを刺されているッス。
English: But before the victim was shot, sir, he was stabbed in the chest.

Japanese: 傷は、かなりヒドかったッスが、 即死にはいたらなかったッス。
English: The wound was very severe, but not enough to cause instantaneous death.

Japanese: 犯人は、トドメを刺すために ピストルを使ったッス。
English: The murderer used the pistol to finish the victim off after the stabbing.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、今のところ、 証言にアヤシイところはない）
English: (Nothing sounds out of place so far...)

Japanese: （今は、凶器に関する 情報を集めよう‥‥）
English: (I should try to learn more about the murder weapons, for now...)

Japanese: 凶器のピストルは、 だれのものだったんですか？
English: The murder weapon, Detective Gumshoe. Whose pistol was it?

Japanese: 被害者、霧崎医師の ものだったッス。
English: It was the victim's.

Japanese: 被害者‥‥？ なぜ、そんなものを‥‥
English: The victim? Now, why would he have...

Japanese: “なぜピストルを持っていたか” ‥‥そんなコトどうでもよろしい。
English: "Why would he have a pistol?" Who cares?

Japanese: それより、そのピストルに 付着していた指紋。
English: The point that you are missing is whose fingerprints are on that pistol.

Japanese: そちらに注目してくれるかしら。 というか、注目しなさい。
English: If you're not already paying attention to that, then I suggest you start.

Japanese: 指紋‥‥ですか。 もしかして‥‥？
English: Fingerprints? There were fingerprints?

Japanese: ピストルのグリップには 被害者の指紋といっしょに、
English: Along with the victim's,

Japanese: 綾里　真宵の指紋が 残されていたッス。
English: the defendant, Maya Fey's were also on the grip, sir.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: 凶器のピストルに、 被告の指紋ですか‥‥。
English: So the defendant's fingerprints were left on the murder weapon...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった‥‥ ワナだったか！）
English: (Hmm... I walked right into her hands there.)

Japanese: 至近距離‥‥って、 どのくらいですか？
English: Point blank, huh? So about how far away was it?

Japanese: ３０～５０ｃｍぐらいッスね。
English: It was anywhere between 12 to 20 inches away.

Japanese: どうして、そんなことが わかるんですか？
English: And how do you know he was shot at point blank?

Japanese: ふう、やれやれ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Tsk, tsk, tsk. Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: バカな国のバカのバカさかげん には、ウンザリするわね‥‥。
English: I grow tired of the foolish foolery of the foolish fools of this foolish country...

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: E-Excuse me!?

Japanese: ‥‥コゲあと。
English: Gunpowder burn.

Japanese: コゲあと‥‥？
English: ...Gunpowder burn?

Japanese: 至近距離で弾丸を撃ちこまれると、 弾痕のまわりに、コゲあとがつく。
English: When something is shot from point blank, a burn area is left around the bullet hole.

Japanese: 弾丸は、火薬の爆発で アツくなるッスから。
English: Gunpowder exploding is what makes a bullet fire, and that gets real hot, pal.

Japanese: 被害者のコメカミは、 ハッキリコゲていたッス！
English: And there were definitely some gunpowder burns left on the victim's forehead!

Japanese: （はあ‥‥。はじめて 聞いたな‥‥）
English: (Wow... Never knew that... Live and learn, I guess...)

Japanese: 刺された‥‥。 その凶器は、どんなものですか？
English: Stabbed... And what was he stabbed with?

Japanese: この果物ナイフッス。
English: A fruit knife.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。そのナイフは どなたの‥‥？
English: I see. And whose knife was it?

Japanese: どうやら、綾里家のもの らしいッス。
English: It looks like it belongs to the Feys, sir.

Japanese: モチロン、綾里　真宵の 指紋がべっとりッス。
English: And of course, Maya Fey's fingerprints are all over it.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥べっとり。
English: Hmm... All over it, huh?

Japanese: （うう‥‥不利だな‥‥）
English: (Urk. This does not look good...)

Japanese: ‥‥ふっふっふっ‥‥ どうしたの？　成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: Ha ha ha. What will you do now, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: どれぐらいの 傷だったんですか？
English: How severe was the wound?

Japanese: あと１センチ右ならば、 シンゾウをぐさりッス。
English: If it had been half an inch more to the right, it would've hit the victim's heart.

Japanese: とても反撃なんてムリッス。 きっと、立ってられなかったッス。
English: After a stab like that, it's impossible to fight back, let alone stand.

Japanese: （カンゼンに、殺意をもって 刺しているな‥‥）
English: (This testimony makes Maya look like she had stabbed him with the intent to kill...)

Japanese: ピストルより、刺された方が先‥‥ まちがいありませんか？
English: Are you sure he was stabbed first, then shot?

Japanese: まちがいないッス。
English: Yup. Sure as sure can be.

Japanese: 傷を調べれば、それぐらいは すぐにわかるッス。
English: One look at the wounds and you'd come to the same conclusion too, pal.

Japanese: バカはバカのバカげた意見を ありがたく聞くものみたいね‥‥。
English: A fool is a fool who will only listen to the foolish opinions of other foolish fools...

Japanese: ‥‥そもそも、コメカミを 至近距離で撃たれれば即死。
English: A pistol shot to the forehead at point blank is certainly enough to kill instantly.

Japanese: ナイフとピストル。 どちらが先か、考えるまでもない。
English: Does it matter, then, which was first?

Japanese: というか、もう少し考えなさい。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Think a little more before you open that big mouth of yours, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （‥‥ムカつくなあ‥‥）
English: (Grr... What a pain...)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 殺害された方法については、 これでハッキリしました。
English: We have clearly established how the victim was murdered.

Japanese: ２つの凶器を、証拠として 提出するッス。
English: I brought the two murder weapons with me today.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 受理しましょう。
English: Very well. The court accepts them into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストル》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナイフ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Knife added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 死亡推定時刻は、６月１９日の 午後３時１５分。
English: The date and time of death was June 19th at 3:15 PM.

Japanese: 証人たちが２発の銃声を聞いた 時刻にまちがいないわ。
English: Eyewitnesses claim to have heard two gunshots at this time.

Japanese: そして、２つの凶器に 被告人の指紋、ですか。
English: And the two murder weapons, both with the defendant's fingerprints on them...?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 まさに、カンペキですな。
English: Hmm... This does seem like an open and shut case.

Japanese: ‥‥トーゼン。
English: Naturally!

Japanese: （まずい‥‥）
English: (This is going from bad to worse...)

Japanese: （事件がシンプルなだけに、 つけいるスキがまったくない！）
English: (As if the summary just now wasn't oversimplifying things to the extreme...)

Japanese: 裁判長。エンリョなく そのハンマーをたたいていいわ。
English: Your Honor. Feel free to slam that little gavel of yours.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、 ギロンの余地はないものね。
English: After all, there is no room left for doubt, is there?

Japanese: たしかにそうですね。 ‥‥弁護人。
English: That is quite true... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: これでもまだ、あなたは 《完全無罪》を主張するのですか？
English: Even in the face of all this, do you still wish to plead "not guilty"?

Japanese: 正直なところ、それでは 勝ち目がないと思いますがね。
English: It's the opinion of this court that if you do not adjust your plea, you stand to lose.

Japanese: 予言は当たったようね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: See, just as I promised, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: １０分以内に、あなたは 意見を変えざるをえなくなる‥‥。
English: You would change your plea in less than ten minutes...

Japanese: どうしますか？　弁護人。
English: What will you do, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 《正当防衛》に主張を変えるなら、 今ならば聞き入れましょう。
English: Will you change to "justified self-defense"? Because now would be the time to do so.

Japanese: これが、最後のチャンスです。
English: This is your final chance.

Japanese: （‥‥この決断は大きい‥‥ よく考えるんだ！）
English: (This is a huge decision! I'd better think this through all the way!)

Japanese: （‥‥有罪になってしまったら、 すべてはおしまいだ）
English: (If Maya is convicted, then it's all over.)

Japanese: （ここは、とにかく真宵ちゃんを 守らなくちゃ‥‥）
English: (I really should play it safe, and try to soften the blow...)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の主張を、 《正当防衛》に‥‥
English: The defense would like to change its plea to "justified self-defense"...
Hah.

Japanese: わかりました。 弁護人の主張を受理しましょう。
English: Understood. Let the record show that the defense has entered a new plea.

Japanese: （‥‥しかたないんだ‥‥）
English: (...There's nothing else I could do...)

Japanese: ‥あなたは、カギをにぎっている‥
English: ...You already hold the key...

Japanese: ‥あなたが信じなければ勝てない‥
English: ...If you don't believe, you cannot win...

Japanese: （《正当防衛》を主張すれば、人を 死なせたと認めたことになる！）
English: (If we plead "justified self- defense", we would basically be confessing to murder!)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、これからの一生を 殺人者として生きることに‥‥）
English: (After the trial, Maya's life would be destroyed, and she'll be labeled a murderer...)

Japanese: （そんなこと‥‥させられるか！）
English: (Can I really... Can I really let that happen!?)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: やはり‥‥主張を 曲げることはできません。
English: The defense... The defense retracts its previous statement.

Japanese: 弁護側は《完全無罪》を なんとしても証明してみせます！
English: And instead, will prove beyond the shadow of a doubt that the defendant is not guilty!

Japanese: （‥‥《正当防衛》を 主張するということは‥‥）
English: (If we plead "justified self- defense"...)

Japanese: （つまり“殺害”を事実として 認めてしまうことになる！）
English: (we would basically be confessing to a murder!)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんはこれからの一生を 殺人者として生きることに‥‥）
English: (After the trial, Maya's life would be destroyed, and she'd be labeled a murderer...)

Japanese: （そんなこと、 できるわけがない！）
English: (I can't let that happen!)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 決断しましたか、弁護人。
English: Have you reached a conclusion, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 弁護側の主張は変わりません。 《完全無罪》以外に、あり得ない！
English: The defense will not change its plea.
We will accept nothing short of complete acquittal!
...You.

Japanese: この瞬間、勝負は決した。 ‥‥刑事。
English: You have sealed your fate, Mr. Phoenix Wright. Detective!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: 最後の証言を。 ‥‥トドメを刺すの。
English: Present the final portion of your testimony -- the final strike.

Japanese: うう‥‥りょ、了解ッス‥‥。
English: Um... Y-Yes... sir...

Japanese: あ、ちょ、ちょっと。 進行役は、この私が‥‥
English: N-Now, see here! Proceedings are run by...

Japanese: ひいっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、いいです。 最後の証言をしてください。
English: Oh, yes, of course. Go ahead Detective and give your testimony...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵の有罪を立証する、 もう１つの証拠について！
English: I think the court would like to hear about the other piece of incriminating evidence!

Japanese: ～真宵の犯行を立証する証拠～
English: -- Incriminating Evidence --

Japanese: ザンネンながら、さらに強力な 証拠があるッス。
English: Sorry, pal but there's an even more incriminating piece of evidence.

Japanese: 被告が現場で着ていた 装束を見てほしいッス。
English: This is the costume the defendant was wearing at the time of the crime.

Japanese: 見てのとおり、血痕が 付着しているッス。
English: As you can see, it's covered in blood.

Japanese: 被告は、明らかに無抵抗の 被害者を殺害したッス。
English: The defendant attacked and killed a person who, without a doubt, was not fighting back.

Japanese: これ‥‥ですか。
English: So this is the costume...

Japanese: たしかに、返り血が ついていますね。
English: There certainly is evidence of a back spray of blood on this.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵が 犯行に関わっている証拠ッス。
English: This piece directly links Maya Fey to the crime, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 受理しましょう。
English: I see... The court accepts this into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《真宵の装束》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Maya's Costume added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （指紋のついた２つの凶器に、 血まみれの装束‥‥）
English: (Maya's fingerprints on both murder weapons and blood splatters on her clothes...)

Japanese: （これ以上サイアクの状況が あり得るか‥‥？）
English: (Could this situation get any worse??)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 コトバもないみたいね。
English: Hah. What's wrong? You seem to be at a loss.

Japanese: 検察側の証言は、 これが最後のはずよ。
English: I think this is the last piece of testimony the prosecution should have to offer.

Japanese: せいぜい、あがくことね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Feel free to sulk off with your tail between your legs, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （たのむから、フルネームで 呼ぶの、やめてくれえ‥‥）
English: (Please stop calling me by my full name. It's disturbing.)

Japanese: ～真宵の犯行を立証する証拠～
English: -- Incriminating Evidence --

Japanese: ザンネンながら、さらに強力な 証拠があるッス。
English: Sorry, pal but there's an even more incriminating piece of evidence.

Japanese: 被告が現場で着ていた 装束を見てほしいッス。
English: This is the costume the defendant was wearing at the time of the crime.

Japanese: 見てのとおり、血痕が 付着しているッス。
English: As you can see, it's covered in blood.

Japanese: 被告は、明らかに無抵抗の 被害者を殺害したッス。
English: The defendant attacked and killed a person who, without a doubt, was not fighting back.

Japanese: （狩魔め‥‥これで一気に 勝負を決める気か）
English: (That Von Karma! She thinks she can decide the verdict with this testimony alone!)

Japanese: （なんとしても‥‥決定的な ムジュンを見つけなければ！）
English: (I have to somehow find a critical contradiction and then I'll have her!)

Japanese: だから、さっさとそれを 証言してください！
English: Why didn't you say so in your testimony earlier!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥今日の弁護人は なんかコワいッス。
English: Uh... You're kinda scary today, you know, pal?

Japanese: ま、まあまあ弁護人。 そうコーフンしないで‥‥。
English: Come now, Mr. Wright... There is no need for that kind of attitude in my court...

Japanese: わわ、わかりました。 そんなにニラまないように。
English: A-Alright. Just, please stop glaring at me like that...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの‥‥装束？
English: Maya's... costume?

Japanese: そッス。ワレワレが かけつけたときに着ていたッス。
English: Yeah. She was wearing this when we arrested her.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥今日も 同じカッコウをしていたな）
English: (Maya... She's wearing her channeling costume today too...)

Japanese: （あれ以外、着ちゃいけない キマリでもあるのかな‥‥？）
English: (Is she not allowed to wear anything else...?)

Japanese: その、装束についている血は‥‥？
English: This blood on the costume...

Japanese: 化学分析の結果、被害者のもので あると断定されているッス。
English: Lab results show that it is the victim's blood.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥被告の装束に、 被害者の血がねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... So there is blood from the victim on the defendant's clothes.

Japanese: （うう‥‥これも かなり不利だぞ‥‥）
English: (Definitely not good... Urk.)

Japanese: あの‥‥他に何か、手がかりに なるようなものは？
English: Hmm, where there any other clues you could gleam from this piece of evidence?

Japanese: そッスね‥‥。
English: Um, well...

Japanese: ‥‥変わったことがあるなら、 とっくに証言している。
English: If you must change the topic, then the good detective here must testify again.

Japanese: 時間のムダ。 ‥‥次、行きなさい。
English: But too bad. Not enough time. Time to move on.

Japanese: 狩魔検事はカンペキ主義 ですからねえ‥‥。
English: Ah, yes, Ms. von Karma is perfectly correct...

Japanese: （‥‥くそ。裁判長も 検事の味方か‥‥）
English: (Grr... Now even the Judge is on her side!)

Japanese: （ヘタに食いさがるのも キケンだけど‥‥どうする！）
English: (But if I bite off more than I can chew here... What should I do!?)

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔検事‥‥、今まで ほとんど口を挟まなかった）
English: (Why is Ms. von Karma suddenly putting up resistance?)

Japanese: （ここで突然の“異議あり”。 何かあるのかもしれない‥‥）
English: (There must be a reason as to why she suddenly threw out an objection like that...)

Japanese: （この装束‥‥何か 手がかりはないのか！）
English: (There must be a clue somewhere on this costume!)

Japanese: （‥‥よく見るんだ‥‥！）
English: (I just have to look harder!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: その装束を穴のあくほど 見つめても、しかたありません。
English: Ms. von Karma's logic is perfect. There is no way for you to poke a hole in it.

Japanese: （くそっ！　時間切れか。 この装束には‥‥）
English: (Argh! Looks like my time is up. So, about the costume...)

Japanese: （深追いして何も出なかったら 致命的だ‥‥）
English: (If I pursue this and I turn up nothing, it could be disastrous...)

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 特にモンダイはありません。
English: You're right, Your Honor. There is absolutely nothing more to this.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、証人。証言をつづけなさい。
English: Good. Well, Detective, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: この証拠品には、 大きな問題があります！
English: Actually, there is something very wrong with this piece of evidence.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ど、どういうことッス！
English: What are you talking about, pal!?

Japanese: い、いったい、何が 問題だと言うのですか！
English: Wh-Where is this problem you are talking about!?

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、目の前に つきつけてやる必要があるな）
English: (I've come this far... There's no turning back now!)

Japanese: この装束がはらんでいる “問題点”とは！
English: The problem I have with this piece of evidence is here!

Japanese: 問題点はもちろん、 この血痕ですよ！
English: The problem is obviously the blood splatter here!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥その血痕は 被害者のものと断定されています。
English: B-But... We've determined that the blood splatter is from the victim.

Japanese: どこがモンダイなのですか？
English: Where is the problem in that?

Japanese: どこッス？
English: Yeah, where, pal?

Japanese: ‥‥どこでしょう。
English: Yeah... I wonder about that too...

Japanese: 今の発言はちょっと カチンと来ました！
English: That last antic of yours just pushed me over the top, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: もう、尋問はこれで 打ち切って‥‥
English: I am declaring that this cross-examination period is over...

Japanese: まま、待ってください！ （今度こそ、はずせないぞ‥‥）
English: P-Please wait, Your Honor! (Ack! I'd better not mess up again...)

Japanese: 問題点‥‥それは、 このへんです！
English: There is a problem... somewhere around... here!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？ ‥‥狩魔検事？
English: Wh-What does he mean, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: ‥‥問題点はそこじゃない。 弁護人の頭脳、ということかしら。
English: There's nothing wrong with it. The only thing wrong here is the defense's head.

Japanese: さすが狩魔検事のムスメさんです。 ‥‥うまいことを言う。
English: Well said. You certainly do take after your father!

Japanese: （バカにしやがって‥‥ もう一度だ！）
English: (I've just been made to look like a fool again... Alright... Focus, Phoenix!)

Japanese: ‥‥この装束の たもとを見てください。
English: I ask the court to please take a look at the sleeve of this costume.

Japanese: たもと‥‥？
English: The sleeve?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ 小さな穴があいています。
English: There is a tiny hole here...

Japanese: ‥‥あ‥‥穴ッスか‥‥？ 報告書には書いてなかったッス。
English: ...A... A hole...? But that wasn't in the report.

Japanese: 待ってください！
English: Hold on!

Japanese: ‥‥この穴のまわり‥‥かすかに、 火薬のニオイがします！
English: What's this around the hole? I-It smells faintly of gunpowder!

Japanese: か‥‥火薬‥‥ 聞いてないッスゥゥ！
English: G-Gunpowder!? No one ever told me!

Japanese: 火薬のニオイがする“穴”‥‥
English: A hole that smells of gunpowder...

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、見つけたぞ！ ヤツらの“穴”を‥‥！）
English: (It looks like I've found the "hole" I was looking for!)

Japanese: 当然それは、弾丸によるもの‥‥ “弾痕”と考えるべきです！
English: Your Honor, the only logical conclusion you can make is that it must be a bullet hole!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥これは、重大なコトです！
English: Order! Order! Order!! This is a very grave matter.

Japanese: どうやら、法廷記録に 情報をくわえたほうがいいですね。
English: It's best we correct the Court Record first before anything else.

Japanese: 証拠品《真宵の装束》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Maya's Costume updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: すす、すまねッス！ ワレワレの見落としッス！
English: Sorry about that. I guess we messed up, sir.

Japanese: （か‥‥狩魔のヤツ‥‥ 笑っている‥‥？）
English: (I-Is she actually... smiling!?)

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥ 知っていたのか！）
English: (What else is she hiding!?)

Japanese: ‥‥ムネをはりなさい、刑事。
English: Pull yourself together, Detective.

Japanese: そんなささいな“穴”。 ‥‥証拠の持つ力は変わらない。
English: That tiny "hole" doesn't change a thing. The strength of the evidence still holds.

Japanese: 証言をつづけて。 今のは“ムジュン”ではないわ。
English: Continue with your testimony. That, just now, was a fluke. Nothing more.

Japanese: ば、バカな！ こんな大きな見落としを‥‥！
English: H-How can you say something like that!? This is a huge oversight!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、これは 警察のミスです。
English: While I agree it is a mistake on the part of the police,

Japanese: しかし、狩魔検事の言うように、 この証拠の発言力は変わりません。
English: what Prosecutor von Karma has said is true. The evidence still stands.

Japanese: もっと決定的な“ムジュン” でもないかぎりは‥‥。
English: If you do not find a more definitive problem with the evidence, then...

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥！
English: No way!!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Detective Gumshoe, please continue with your testimony.
Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: 血痕は被害者のものだった。 ‥‥それはわかりました。
English: Alright, I'll give you that the blood splatter is from the victim, but...

Japanese: でも、たもとにあいた 穴は、なんなんですか！
English: what is this hole in the sleeve?

Japanese: うう‥‥すまねッス。ワレワレの 目がフシ穴だったということで‥‥
English: Um... Sorry, pal. We must've thought it was a tear in the seam...

Japanese: そんなオチをつけてすむ モンダイじゃありません！
English: Missing the hole is not what I'm asking you about. I'm talking about the hole itself!

Japanese: 火薬のニオイがする‥‥
English: The smell of gunpowder clearly gives away that...

Japanese: アキラカに弾痕です！
English: it is a bullet hole!

Japanese: ‥‥どうでもいいでしょ、 そんなこと。
English: As if little things like that matter.

Japanese: え。
English: But they do.

Japanese: 装束には、“弾痕”といっしょに “血痕”も残っている。
English: That there is a "bullet hole" in the sleeve and blood all over the costume...

Japanese: ‥‥被告が被害者を殺害したという 事実は、変えられないってコト。
English: it doesn't change the fact that the defendant killed the victim.

Japanese: （やっぱり、証言のムジュン点を 探すしかないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I know there's something more to this costume.)

Japanese: さあ、刑事。 さっさとすませちゃって。
English: Come now, Detective. You're wasting precious time.

Japanese: ‥‥了解ッス。
English: ...Yes, sir.

Japanese: “無抵抗だった”‥‥ どうしてそれがわかるんですか！
English: He wasn't "fighting back"? How do you know if he was or wasn't!?

Japanese: 被害者に、抵抗した跡が 見られないからッス。
English: We could find no evidence that the victim put up any sort of struggle, pal.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 被害者と争っていたのか、 犯人が一方的に殺害したのか‥‥
English: So did the murderer have a fight with the victim, or not?

Japanese: これは、まったく意味あいが ちがってきますからねえ。
English: Depending on this, the circumstances around this murder change drastically.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ またまた大ピンチか‥‥）
English: (Ugh... We're in real trouble now...)

Japanese: （“被害者が無抵抗じゃなかった” という証拠でもあればなあ‥‥）
English: (If only I had something to prove that the victim did fight back...)

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事！
English: Detective Dick Gumshoe!

Japanese: な‥‥なんスか？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: フルネームで呼ばれると、 なんかちょっとブキミッス‥‥。
English: Heh, having you call me by my full name is kind of a weird feeling...

Japanese: “無抵抗な被害者を殺害した” ‥‥そう証言しましたね？
English: You said that my client killed "a person who, without a doubt, was not fighting back."

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yeah, I did.

Japanese: じゃあ、警察が見落とした この弾痕は、どうなるんですか！
English: Then what, may I ask, is the bullet hole you police overlooked supposed to mean!?

Japanese: え‥‥ ど、どういうコトッスか？
English: Eh, um... What does it mean?

Japanese: いいですか。
English: I'll tell you what it means.

Japanese: 弾痕があるということは、 被害者は発砲したわけです。
English: It means that the victim had fired off a shot.

Japanese: これのどこが “無抵抗の被害者”なんですかッ！
English: Is this what it means to "not fight back"?

Japanese: や‥‥やられたッスゥゥ！
English: A-Ah! You're right!

Japanese: たしかに、そのとおりです！
English: It would seem that way!

Japanese: 被告に向かって発砲したとなると、 状況は変わってきますね‥‥。
English: If the victim tried to shoot the defendant, then... It would change everything...

Japanese: （やったぞ！ これで、風向きが‥‥）
English: (Alright! The wind seems to be shifting...)
Hah.

Japanese: （なんだ？　あの“ごくろうさま” と言わんばかりのタメイキは？）
English: (What is with that "are you finished yet" laugh?)

Japanese: ごくろうさま。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Are you finished yet, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？ 狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥綾里　真宵は、 被害者に撃たれたみたいね。
English: It seems... that Maya Fey was shot at by the victim.

Japanese: ‥‥それなら、《正当防衛》が 成立するかもしれない。
English: However, that is only grounds enough to support a "justified self-defense" plea.

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: でも‥‥おぼえてるでしょ？ そこの弁護士は、ハッキリ言った。
English: But I'm sure you remember, Your Honor, what the defense clearly said.

Japanese: ‥‥《正当防衛》ではなく、 《完全無罪》を主張する、って。
English: They rejected "justified self- defense" and pleaded "not guilty."

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: Now that you...

Japanese: そうでした！
English: Why, that's right!

Japanese: つまり！
English: Which means!

Japanese: 弁護側はまだ、 何も証明していない！
English: The defense has yet to prove anything at all!

Japanese: そんなあアァッ！
English: Nooooo!!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに、 そのとおりです‥‥。
English: W-Well... Yes, that's true...

Japanese: ‥‥それに。
English: Furthermore...

Japanese: 装束に弾痕が あったからといって、
English: just the fact that there is a bullet hole in the costume...

Japanese: それで《正当防衛》が 証明されるわけじゃない。
English: is not enough to substantiate even a plea of "justified self-defense".

Japanese: え。そうなんですか‥‥？
English: Huh? How so?

Japanese: はぎゃッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ‥‥ボケッとしてないで、
English: Don't just stand there.

Japanese: さっさと事件当日に 起こったことを再現なさい。
English: Hurry up and tell the court what transpired that day.

Japanese: ‥‥新しく発見された データもおりこんで。
English: ...With the new information we acquired added in, of course.

Japanese: え‥‥？ じ、自分が‥‥ッスか。
English: Huh? You mean... By myself...?

Japanese: 自分はその、アタマを 使うのがあの‥‥
English: You want me to put together the scenario all by myself?

Japanese: うはっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: やら、やらせていただくッス！
English: Y-Yes, sir! Right away, sir!

Japanese: ～推測される事件の再現～
English: -- What Transpired --

Japanese: 被告は霊媒中、被害者のスキを見て ナイフでムネを刺したッス。
English: During the channeling, the defendant saw her chance to stab the victim in the chest.

Japanese: 被害者は当然、最後の力を ふりしぼって、抵抗したッス。
English: Of course, the victim used the last of his strength to fight back, sir.

Japanese: ２人はもみ合って、被害者は ピストルを取り出したッス。
English: While the two were fighting, the victim took out his gun.

Japanese: 被害者は発砲したッスが、 距離が近すぎて、はずしたッス。
English: The victim took a shot, but because they were too close, he missed.

Japanese: そのスキに被告はピストルを 奪って、トドメを‥‥。
English: The defendant then picked up on the opening, took the victim's gun and ended it...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ スジはとおっていますね。
English: Hmm... This scenario you have put together does make sense...

Japanese: ホッ‥‥。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: 弁護人、どうですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （たしかに、スジはとおっている ように聞こえる‥‥）
English: (Just by listening, it does make sense...)

Japanese: （でも、ゼッタイに 認めるワケにはいかない！）
English: (However, I won't give up that easily!)

Japanese: そそ、そんなにニラまないように。
English: P-Please refrain from glaring at me like that...

Japanese: ‥‥では、尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, your cross- examination, please.

Japanese: ～推測される事件の再現～
English: -- What Transpired --

Japanese: 被告は霊媒中、被害者のスキを見て ナイフでムネを刺したッス。
English: During the channeling, the defendant saw her chance to stab the victim in the chest.

Japanese: 被害者は当然、最後の力を ふりしぼって、抵抗したッス。
English: Of course, the victim used the last of his strength to fight back, sir.

Japanese: ２人はもみ合って、被害者は ピストルを取り出したッス。
English: While the two were fighting, the victim took out his gun.

Japanese: 被害者は発砲したッスが、 距離が近すぎて、はずしたッス。
English: The victim took a shot, but because they were too close, he missed.

Japanese: そのスキに被告はピストルを 奪って、トドメを‥‥。
English: The defendant then picked up on the opening and took the victim's gun and ended it...

Japanese: （‥‥ニクらしいほど もっともらしく聞こえる）
English: (His testimony certainly makes us look very bad,)

Japanese: （でも、どこかにムジュンを 見つけないと‥‥）
English: (but there's gotta be a contradiction in there somewhere...)

Japanese: （それも、決定的なヤツを！）
English: (And uncovering it is going to uncover the truth! I can feel it!)

Japanese: 霧崎医師と被告人は、 体格にかなり差があります。
English: There is quite a difference in height between Dr. Grey and the defendant.

Japanese: 被告人の体力では、 被害者を刺すのはムリです！
English: Add in body strength, and it seems unlikely the defendant could have stabbed the victim!

Japanese: そ‥‥そういえば そッスねえ‥‥。
English: Now that you mention it... Yeah, I guess...

Japanese: ‥‥トボける気かしら？ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: You think you can get away with such flimsy reasoning? Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は霊媒中だった。 ‥‥そうでしょ？
English: Maya Fey was in the middle of channeling, was she not?

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道では、 術者は交霊中、体格も変化する。
English: When channeling with the Kurain Channeling Technique, the medium physically changes.

Japanese: 看護師の体格ならば、霧崎医師と 争えたはずよ。
English: With the nurse's build, the defendant could have easily been a match for the doctor.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥そんなことまで 調べてるのか‥‥！）
English: (I don't believe it... She even studied up on the Kurain Channeling Technique!)

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずよ。 私は、カンペキなの。
English: Like I said before... I am perfect.

Japanese: あのお‥‥今の話、 私にはその、イミが‥‥
English: Um... Uh... About what you were talking about... I didn't quite get it...

Japanese: な、なんでもないです。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Um, n-never mind. Let's continue with the testimony...

Japanese: ナイフで刺されただけでは 死ななかったわけですね。
English: So he was stabbed, but the stab wound didn't kill him.

Japanese: ただ、出血量から考えて、 かなりダメージがあったッス。
English: But if you think about the blood loss, it was pretty bad.

Japanese: どれぐらいのダメージですか？
English: How bad would you say it was?

Japanese: そうそう。じつはこないだ、 献血に行ってきたッス。
English: Actually, I went to give blood the other day.

Japanese: 終わったら、ちょっと くらくらっとしたッス。
English: And afterward, I felt a little lightheaded and dizzy.

Japanese: ‥‥あれの１０倍ぐらい、 くらくらしたんじゃないかと‥‥
English: ...I guess the damage was maybe... about 10 times the dizziness...?

Japanese: きゃん！
English: Ack!

Japanese: あ‥‥あいすまねッス！
English: S-Sorry!

Japanese: そのピストルは、 いったいどこから‥‥？
English: Where in the world did that pistol come from?

Japanese: どうやら被害者の霧崎医師は、 あの日のために用意していたッス。
English: It looks like the victim, Dr. Grey had specifically bought it for that day.

Japanese: ピストルを、ですか‥‥？
English: But a handgun...?

Japanese: ヤミルートから手に入れたッス。 事件の２日前に。
English: He got it off the black market about 2 days before the murder.

Japanese: （霧崎先生、どうして ピストルなんか‥‥）
English: (Why did Dr. Grey bring a gun...?)

Japanese: （用心のため、 だったのかな‥‥？）
English: (Was he taking precautions against something...?)

Japanese: じゃあ、この装束の弾痕は、 そのときに‥‥？
English: So, you're saying that the bullet hole in this costume was made then?

Japanese: そう考えるのが自然ッス。
English: Sorry, pal, but that's what I'd think.

Japanese: ２人はもみ合っていて、 そのとき発砲されたッス。
English: The two of them were already fighting when a shot was fired.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん、当たらなくて よかったな‥‥）
English: (Maya... I'm really glad you weren't hurt...)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、ピストルなんて 撃ったことはない！
English: Maya has never fired a gun before in her life!

Japanese: 安全装置は、被害者が すでにハズしていたッス。
English: The victim had already taken off the safety.

Japanese: 引き金さえ引けば、アンタですら 発砲できたッス。
English: With the safety off, even an amateur like you can fire it by pulling the trigger.

Japanese: （そうなのか‥‥ ぼくですら‥‥）
English: (Even me...? I wonder...?)

Japanese: おわかりかしら？
English: Now do you understand?

Japanese: 狩魔のロジックは カンペキなの。
English: A Von Karma's logic is perfect.

Japanese: ‥‥装束にあいた、小さな穴。
English: Missing the tiny hole on this costume...

Japanese: これを見落としたのは 致命的でしたね。
English: will be the prosecution's undoing.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトッスか？
English: Eh? What do you mean?

Japanese: この小さな穴が、あなたの証言に あいた大きな穴を証明している！
English: This little hole has actually created a huge hole in your testimony!

Japanese: は‥‥早く説明を！ 成歩堂くん！
English: E-Explain yourself, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ２人はもみ合っていて、被害者が 至近距離から発砲した。
English: You said the two of them were fighting when the victim fired his gun at point blank.

Japanese: それならばなぜ！ 装束の弾痕にコゲあとがないのか？
English: If that were true, then where is the gunpowder burn on this costume!?

Japanese: こ‥‥コゲあと‥‥ッスか‥‥？
English: G-Gunpowder... burn?

Japanese: さっき、あなたは こう証言したはずです！
English: This is what you testified earlier:

Japanese: “至近距離から撃たれた弾痕には、 コゲあとができる”と！
English: "When something is shot from point blank, a burn area is left around the bullet hole."

Japanese: うおおッ！
English: OH!

Japanese: この装束の弾痕には、 コゲあとなんか、ない！
English: But there is not a single trace of gunpowder burn on this costume!!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そのとおりです。
English: That is a very good point.

Japanese: となると‥‥どういうことに？
English: And... what exactly does this mean?

Japanese: 発砲されたとき、２人はかなり はなれていたことになります。
English: It means that when the shot was fired, they were standing apart from each other.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ガッカリね、成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: I'm disappointed, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: そんなことで、私のロジックに 穴があくと思って？
English: You think you can punch a hole in my logic with that?

Japanese: そのていどのコト、 なんとでも説明がつくでしょ！
English: With wishy-washy thinking like that, anyone can explain anything away!

Japanese: それなら、なんとでも 説明してもらいましょう。
English: Then I implore you to disprove my line of thinking.

Japanese: たとえば、そうね‥‥。
English: Let's see...

Japanese: もみ合っているときに、 被害者が被告人を突きとばした。
English: In the middle of their fight, the victim pushed the defendant away.

Japanese: そして、距離があいたときに、 撃った！　‥‥どう？
English: And it was then, when they were separated, that he fired! How was that?

Japanese: ‥‥お話になりませんね。
English: As if that was even possible.

Japanese: 証言では、被害者は刺されたとき、 かなりダメージを受けていた。
English: According to testimony, the wound from the stabbing was very severe.

Japanese: 被告人を遠くまで突きとばす チカラなど、残っていない！
English: The victim would not have had the strength to push the defendant very far after that!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥そうね。
English: W-Well... Then...

Japanese: ‥‥そう！ 被告のほうから、はなれた！
English: That's right! The defendant must have pushed the victim away!

Japanese: ナイフで刺したあと、彼女は 一度、霧崎医師とキョリをとった。
English: After stabbing him, she must have put some space between the doctor and herself.

Japanese: そして、ナイフをかまえなおして、 ふたたび、おそいかかったのよ。
English: And then while she was preparing to strike again, the doctor took his shot.

Japanese: そのとき、被害者は発砲した。 ‥‥これなら問題ないでしょう！
English: There. That should satisfy even you.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥それなら スジがとおるようです。
English: Hmm... That does make an awful lot of sense.

Japanese: いかがですか？　成歩堂くん。
English: What do you think, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （ここは‥‥マチガイは ゆるされないぞ！）
English: (I must be careful. I can't afford to make a mistake here!)

Japanese: （ジックリ急いで考えるんだ！）
English: (Concentrate and think!)

Japanese: （‥‥どう考えても‥‥今度こそ “穴”はないみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Ack, it's no good. I can't find a "hole" in her logic at all this time...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ スジはとおるようです。
English: I admit it. Somehow, your reasoning makes logical sense.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！ ‥‥そのコトバが聞きたかったの。
English: That's it. That's exactly what I wanted to hear from you, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: １１時。お茶の時間は すぎてしまったようね‥‥。
English: Drat. It's 11:00 AM. I've already missed my morning tea.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: ...However.

Japanese: とにかく、今度こそ ケツロンが出たみたいね。
English: At least we were able to arrive at a conclusion.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そのようですな。
English: Hmm... It does look that way.

Japanese: （‥‥だ、ダメだ！　このままじゃ すべてが終わってしまう！）
English: (...Oh boy... If I don't do something soon, it'll be curtains for Maya...)

Japanese: それでは、これで‥‥
English: Well then, I think we can...

Japanese: 裁判長‥‥お待ちください！
English: Your Honor! Please... Wait!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？　弁護人？ 発言は慎重におねがいします！
English: Who was that? Mr. Wright? Please refrain from making unnecessary outbursts!

Japanese: （ムリヤリでも、狩魔の説明に ムジュンを見つけるんだ！）
English: (I don't care how, but I must find something to go on in Von Karma's explanation!)

Japanese: 狩魔検事の説明には、 致命的なムジュンがあります！
English: There is a fatal flaw in her argument, Your Honor!

Japanese: 致命的な‥‥
English: Fatal...?

Japanese: ムジュン‥‥！ やはっ！
English: Flaw...!? Yeow!

Japanese: おもしろい‥‥。 じゃ、見せてもらおうじゃない！
English: Very interesting. I would love to see where this "flaw" is!

Japanese: 私の説明に ムジュンする証拠品を！
English: Show me something that contradicts my explanation!

Japanese: （‥‥さっきの狩魔の説明には、 どこかにムジュンがあるはず！）
English: (There has to be a snag in her explanation somewhere!)

Japanese: （“被告は、トドメを刺す前に 被害者とキョリをとった”）
English: ("She put some distance between them before rushing to make the final blow.")

Japanese: （“そしてもう一度、おそい かかったところを撃たれた”）
English: ("And when she was about to strike, the doctor took his shot.")

Japanese: （‥‥この説明にムジュンする 証拠品は、ズバリ！）
English: (There must be a piece of evidence that contradicts this line of thinking!)

Japanese: これを見てください！
English: Take a look at this!

Japanese: それがどうしたッス？
English: Why are you showing this to me, pal?

Japanese: あきらかに、狩魔検事の 説明のムジュンを‥‥
English: Prosecutor von Karma's explanation and this piece of evidence...

Japanese: 説明していないッス！
English: ...Do not contradict!
...Huh?

Japanese: やれやれ。考えなおしたほうが いいッスな。
English: Haha, looks like you need to use your brain a little more, pal.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事にあしらわれると、 ヒドくクツジョクを感じるな）
English: (Ugh... Being lectured by Gumshoe, of all people, is so embarrassing...)

Japanese: ホラ！　早くやりなおすッス！
English: Come on! Hurry up and get that thinking cap on!

Japanese: その証拠は‥‥これです。
English: This is the piece of evidence that destroys your logic.

Japanese: なんですか‥‥ビョウブ？
English: What is that? A folding screen?

Japanese: 重要なのは、ビョウブに あいている“穴”です。
English: I would like to point the court's attention to the "hole" in this folding screen.

Japanese: ‥‥あああッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 わかったようですね。
English: It looks like you already know what I'm talking about.

Japanese: え？　え？　何がッス？
English: Who? Where? What?

Japanese: せせ、セツメイを！
English: Mr. Wright! Your explanation, please!

Japanese: （‥‥このヒトたち、 ホントにわからないのか‥‥？）
English: (Are these two really that clueless...?)

Japanese: 弾丸は、被告のたもとをつらぬいた あと、ビョウブに当たった。
English: The bullet went through the defendant's sleeve first, then the folding screen.

Japanese: 床から２０ｃｍほどのところに。 ‥‥ということは！
English: It passed through at a height of approximately 8 inches off the ground... Which means!

Japanese: 発砲されたとき、真宵ちゃん‥‥ いや被告は‥‥
English: When the shot was fired, Maya... I mean, the defendant,

Japanese: おそいかかるどころか、 しゃがみこんでいたことになる！
English: was not getting ready to strike, but was actually squatting low to the ground!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥た、たしかに そういうことになります！
English: Th-This changes everything!

Japanese: 現場の上面図を見てください。
English: Please look at this diagram of the crime scene.

Japanese: 銃を撃ったとき、霧崎医師は このあたりにいました。
English: The victim, Dr. Grey was here when he fired the shot.

Japanese: そして、弾丸はビョウブに 当たりました。
English: And the bullet hit this folding screen.

Japanese: ‥‥ビョウブのこの位置です。 高さは、床から２０ｃｍ。
English: It hit at this location, about 8 inches off the ground.

Japanese: その瞬間‥‥、被告は このあたりにいたはずです。
English: At this time, the defendant was in this area.

Japanese: このあたりです！
English: Here!

Japanese: ビョウブのウラ‥‥ですか？
English: Behind the folding screen...?

Japanese: ぎゃあ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: 被害者と犯人は、もみあって いたんでしょ！
English: The victim and his attacker were fighting, were they not?

Japanese: なんでそんなところに いるのよ！
English: Then what would the attacker be doing all the way back there!?
Um...

Japanese: だいいち、ビョウブのウラに いたんじゃあ、
English: Besides which, if the attacker was behind the folding screen,

Japanese: 被害者が、それをねらって 撃てたはずがない！
English: then how could the victim even know where to shoot? It's obviously impossible!

Japanese: そりゃそうですね。
English: Yeah, I guess so...

Japanese: コラ！　弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （さすがにマズかったな‥‥）
English: (Boy, did I just screw up royally...)

Japanese: （とにかく、事件が起こったときの 真宵ちゃんの位置が重要だ！）
English: (What's done is done. For now, I should focus on where Maya was at the time of the crime.)

Japanese: ここらへんです！
English: Somewhere around here!

Japanese: （マズい！　裁判長、シリが カユそうなカオをしてる‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. The judge's face is scrunched like he just smelled some serious bad breath...)

Japanese: え、えーと‥‥ あるいはですね。
English: Uhh... Um... That is, I mean...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ ‥‥おシリがカユいもので。
English: Wait! I just realized I forgot to brush this morning... Excuse me while I freshen up.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Uh, sure...

Japanese: その間にもう一度、 あなたも考えなおしなさい！
English: In the meantime, I want you to think things through again, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （やっぱり、 ちがってたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Ah, of course... Whoops...)

Japanese: （とにかく、事件が起こったときの 真宵ちゃんの位置が重要だ！）
English: (What's done is done. For now, I should focus on where Maya was at the time of the crime.)

Japanese: もちろんここ。 ビョウブの近くです！
English: She was standing here, near the folding screen!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい！
English: W-Wait a second!

Japanese: 被害者がコシを低くしていたのは、 弾痕の高さから推測できる。
English: We know the defendant was close to the ground based on the height of the bullet hole.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、被害者とのキョリは どうなの！
English: But... How can you gauge the distance from that!?

Japanese: もっと、被害者と近い位置に いたかもしれないじゃない！
English: Isn't it possible that the defendant was standing much closer to the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥それはあり得ません。
English: That's impossible.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ！
English: B-But why!?

Japanese: 理由は、きみが一番よく 知ってるはずだ、狩魔検事。
English: You, of all people, should know the answer to that question, Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: 近いキョリから撃たれた 弾痕には、コゲあとができる。
English: If she were shot from somewhere closer, there would be gunpowder burns present.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: ‥‥この装束の弾痕に、 そんなものは見あたらない！
English: There is nothing of the sort around the bullet hole of this costume!

Japanese: ああああッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: く‥‥この‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一が‥‥ッ！
English: C-Curse you, Mr. Phoenix Wright! You...!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: “被害者が発砲した瞬間 被告は、はなれた場所にいた”
English: I believe it has now been proven that

Japanese: それは立証されたようです。
English: the defendant was standing a ways from the victim when she was shot at.

Japanese: しかし、被告の状況は 変わらないと思いますが‥‥？
English: But do you think this has changed the defendant's situation...?

Japanese: （そう言われると、 変わらない気もするな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, even with that explanation, it feels like nothing has changed...)

Japanese: （しかし、ここでそれを 認めるわけにはいかない！）
English: (But I can't give up just yet!)

Japanese: 冗談じゃありません！ 状況は、まったく変わります！
English: Honestly, Your Honor! This changes everything!

Japanese: いいですか！　被害者は刺されて 死にかけていた！
English: The prosecution has claimed that the defendant was aiming to kill by stabbing.

Japanese: もし殺害するつもりならば、 ナイフでトドメを刺せばいい。
English: If that were true, delivering the final strike with the knife would be ideal.

Japanese: しかし！　ジッサイは どうだったでしょう‥‥？
English: However! Where and what was the defendant doing at the time...?

Japanese: ‥‥被告はビョウブのそばで‥‥ しゃがんでいたッス‥‥。
English: ...Squatting all the way by the folding screen...?

Japanese: そのとおり‥‥。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: もし綾里　真宵が犯人ならば、
English: If Maya Fey was the real murderer,

Japanese: なぜトドメを刺さず、ビョウブの そばでしゃがみこんでいたのか？
English: why would she be by the folding screen instead of preparing to strike?
Nnngh...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥不可解ですねえ。
English: Upon further consideration, it does make very little sense.

Japanese: きっと、何か事情があったッス。
English: Yeah, I figured there had to be a reason.

Japanese: その“事情”を立証するのが あなたの仕事です！
English: Figuring things out and proving the logic behind everything is YOUR job!

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥！
English: Oof...

Japanese: （よし！　これで判決は カンゼンに‥‥）
English: (Alright! With this, the rest of the trial should be in the b--)

Japanese: （判決はカンゼンに‥‥ わからなくなったな）
English: (...blast radius of disaster.)

Japanese: さすがね‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You are such a smart man, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 何もないゼロの状況から、 ここまで持ちなおすとは。
English: To think that you've been able to take a completely hopeless case to this point...

Japanese: パパを手こずらせただけの ことはある。楽しいわ‥‥。
English: Now I know why papa had a tough time with you. Mmm, you amuse me...

Japanese: （出た‥‥オヤジゆずりの イヤミなウス笑い‥‥）
English: (Uuugh. Of all the things to inherit, why did it have to be that smarmy smile!?)
Detective.

Japanese: よくも、私のカンペキな ロジックをキズつけてくれたわね。
English: How dare you damage my perfect logic!?

Japanese: え、え！　‥‥自分のせいッスか？
English: H-Huh!?... How is it all my fault??

Japanese: とりあえず、その中途ハンパな ヒゲをそっておきなさい。
English: You can start repairing your standing by first removing that three-strand "goatee".

Japanese: おしおきは、そのあと。
English: Oh, and rest assured your punishment will come later.

Japanese: ‥‥お、おしおき‥‥
English: ...P-Punishment...

Japanese: じゃ、裁判長。 この刑事のカオも見あきたし、
English: Well then, Your Honor, I think I've had all I can take of this Detective's face.

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ 次の証人を呼ばせてもらうわ。
English: I think it's time to call in the next witness.

Japanese: （次の証人‥‥？ そうか、ナツミさんだな！）
English: (Next witness...? That's gotta be Lotta!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ここで、５分間の休憩を とりましょう。
English: The court will take a 5 minute recess.

Japanese: 次の証人の話を聞くのは、 それからにします。
English: After we reconvene, we will hear from the next witness.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、綾里　真宵に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maya Fey...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
